heroHeadline: Hubble Turns 36; Space Community Pushes Back on 47% NASA Science Cut
heroSubtitle: Apr 24: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope marks its 36th anniversary of launch as the scientific community responds to the White House's proposed FY2027 budget — a 23% overall NASA cut with a 47% reduction to the Science Mission Directorate. Following Administrator Isaacman's April 22 congressional testimony, the Planetary Society, AAS, and House Science Committee Chair Babin (R-TX) registered bipartisan opposition to the proposal, which would cancel or delay multiple flagship science missions while increasing Artemis exploration funding. At KSC, the 30-day Orion heat shield inspection continues at the MPPF with sample extraction underway; results expected ~May 10 remain the critical input for the Artemis IV crewed lunar landing (2028) timeline.
footerNote: Data sourced from NASA.gov, OIG Reports, SpaceNews, Ars Technica, Congressional appropriations. Tier 1: NASA/ESA/Official. Tier 2: SpaceNews/Ars Technica/Reuters. Tier 3: Planetary Society/AIAA.
